channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene has brought climate change to the forefront of the presidential campaign after the issue lingered on the margins for months.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Emhoff denies report he slapped ex-girlfriend outside ritzy overseas movie event
item.description: Second gentleman Doug Emhoff denied the veracity of a Daily Mail report in which three unnamed sources accused him of having slapped a former girlfriend during a 2012 trip to the Cannes Film Festival.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minneapolis mayor, city council clash on homeless shelter funding
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council is launching an inquiry into the mayor’s financial reporting practices following a dispute over funding for a homeless shelter.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump and the federal election case against him: Key passages from prosecutors' latest court filing
item.description: New court papers in Donald Trump’s federal 2020 election interference case pull back the curtain on his conversations with family members, former Vice President Mike Pence and other close advisers in the weeks leading up to the attack on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Farnoush Amiri, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden student loan forgiveness plan gets win in Georgia court
item.description: The ruling came one day before the temporary restraining order would expire, allowing the administration to continue the program while the case is moved to Missouri.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's campaign says he raised $160 million in September, ended month with $283 million banked
item.description: Former President Donald Trump has raised $160 million for his campaign in September and entered October with $283 million in the bank for the campaign’s final sprint.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What's next after prosecutors reveal new evidence in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Special counsel Jack Smith has provided a road map for how prosecutors hope to prove their case charging former President Donald Trump with an illegal scheme to overturn his 2020 election loss — if it ever gets to trial.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Debate performance gives Vance 2028 White House boost
item.description: Sen. JD Vance’s (R-Ohio) debate performance is giving him a shot in the arm to become a leading 2028 presidential contender no matter what happens in November between former President Trump and Vice President Harris.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: 8 House Dems demand to know how social platforms are preparing to combat 'misinformation' and 'disinformation'
item.description: Eight Democratic representatives issued a letter to social media executives this week demanding clarification how their platforms will protect against "misinformation" and "disinformation" during the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voters in critical Michigan county focused on economy, candidate character as 2024 race tightens
item.description: Voters in Kent County, Michigan, a swing county in one of the seven key swing states, expressed some doubts about both former President Trump and Vice President Kamala Harris.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump targets Biden, Harris over federal response to hurricane: 'Incompetently managed'
item.description: As President Biden spends a second straight day in the Southeast surveying storm damage from Hurricane Helene, former President Trump blasts President Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris over the federal government's relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Melania Trump: ‘We are dealing with much more danger’
item.description: Former first lady Melania Trump weighed in on the state of former President Donald Trump's bid for the White House in an interview Wednesday, while also reflecting on the recent assassination attempts on her husband's life.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Giuliani's daughter: ‘The biggest threat to our country is Trump’
item.description: Former New York City Mayor Rudy Giuliani's (R) daughter said she thinks the biggest threat to America is former President Trump. Caroline Giuliani has spoken out in recent days about her father’s entanglements with Trump and the upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 key details in special counsel Jack Smith's Trump election case filing
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith argues in a new court filing that former President Donald Trump is not immune from prosecution for his conduct immediately after the 2020 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Early voting begins in downtown Chicago
item.description: Early voting for the November election begins Thursday in downtown Chicago, with DuPage County seeing nearly double the amount of voters who turned out early in 2020, and early voting in all 50 wards opens up next Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: NYC Schools Chancellor Banks to step down sooner than expected
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Schools Chancellor David Banks is expected to step down from his position sooner than expected, several months before he was supposed to retire, a City Hall spokesperson confirmed to PIX11 News on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: How Black leaders in New York are grappling with Eric Adams and representation
item.description: The indictment of New York City mayor Eric Adams on federal bribery charges has sparked debates among Black leaders and community advocates about whether to stand behind a figure who, for many, represents a milestone of Black representation in government.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Morrison And Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Prosecutors lay out new evidence in Trump election case, accuse him of having 'resorted to crimes'
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump laid the groundwork to try to overturn the 2020 election even before he lost, knowingly pushed false claims of voter fraud and “resorted to crimes” in his failed bid to cling to power, according to a court filing unsealed Wednesday that offers new evidence from the lan
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The fight to recover continues for Florida farmers after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Florida farmers are facing significant challenges in their recovery following Hurricane Helene’s devastation.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kaine and Cao face off in only debate of campaign for US Senate seat from Virginia
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — U.S. Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia and Republican challenger Hung Cao sparred over issues from illegal immigration to tariffs on foreign goods Wednesday in their only debate of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris will tour Helene devastation in Georgia, North Carolina as storm scrambles campaign schedule
item.description: The Democratic nominee plans to visit Georgia on Wednesday and survey the devastation in North Carolina in the coming days, according to aides.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Reviewing the Vance-Walz vice-presidential debate
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis talks with political analysts about Tuesday night’s vice-presidential debate between Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Gillibrand vs. Sapraicone: The Race for US Senate forum
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand and Republican candidate Mike Sapraicone faced off in a Senate forum that aired on PIX11 News Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: How young Republicans and Democrats are whipping up votes in key districts
item.description: As the presidential race ramps up in Georgia, one vital voting demographic is mobilizing and hoping to impact the race: young people.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly, Erika Ryan, Kira Wakeam, and Courtney Dorning, MPR News

item.title: Harris trolls Trump at Vegas rally and LA fundraiser, says her crowds are 'pretty big'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris poked at Donald Trump over crowd sizes, his refusal to debate again and his privileged background on Sunday as she hauled in campaign cash in California and held a raucous rally at the same Nevada venue where the Republican nominee had appeared just two
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon senator introduces bill to reform Supreme Court, add 6 more seats
item.description: Oregon Sen. Ron Wyden introduced bill on Thursday that would bring fundamental changes to the nation's high court.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, Fox 5
